reviews.review_title: Could be a 5 star game, but...
reviews.review_description:
I really want to love this game. It has everything I want in a vr ttrpg, 
 great minis and dioramas,  fun customizable characters,  good storyline. Unfortunately it is just too glitchy to give it full stars. The combat is supposed to be real time with pause ( which I love) but it is really difficult to select the character you want to activate with the thumbstick, so you pretty much have to just play it in auto pause mode and just scroll randomly through your roster until you get the player you want and then take your actions. A bit tedious. 
And the game often just bugs out and leaves you with a completed campaign and no way to continue.  Or worse still just randomly resets on you ( I had beaten all 6 campaigns and was playing in the design mode just collecting the leaders crowns. I had 4 of them and then at the end of the Queen Shaygrish campaign instead of giving me her crown and continuing.  The game just reset!) 
Maybe if the devs get all the bugs worked out this will be a great game. As is, it is a good game, but with some frustrations

reviews.review_title: A great game and fantastic use of MR
reviews.review_description:
Successor is a miniature RPG played in a diorama on a 3D tiled board, blending real-time and turn-based strategy in a way that feels natural and engaging. I found that experiencing Successor in mixed reality to be especially impressive. It feels like I'm living in the future playing this game that is floating right in front of me in my living space! The board itself can be easily and precisely positioned for comfort and ease. It took a little time to learn the mechanics of the game, but once everything clicked the experience becomes fluid and intuitive. There is a unique skill tree for each character, a wide variety of weapons that become unlocked as you play, various items, loot, lots of different enemies, detailed environments and gameplay decisions that add meaningful depth and replay-ability. I've probably played close to 30 hours so far and I'm still really enjoying the game. I play on my couch and in bed, and I find it to be engaging and relaxing, a great way to wind down after a day of work or school. Overall, Successor is a standout title and one of the most enjoyable VR experiences I’ve had this year - especially in mixed reality.

Update 10-20: I'm still really hooked by this game and have been playing most days since I purchased it about a month ago, typically in 1.5 hour increments after work. I'm guessing I've played close to 50 hours all together. I completed the main mission after playing about 35 hours and I've been questing for more unlockable items since. For me, this is the most that I've played any game this year. The more I play the more I appreciate how well designed this game is, in function and aesthetically.

If you're a fan of tabletop games with minis and some board game elements thrown in; like I do, you're going to love this game. The beatufully crafted map colors, and the tiles, really pop in VR, they look hand painted... the minis look good like they were hand painted as well. I love the fact it's in mixed reality and it doesn't require a internet connection, that's all a plus, plus, for me. 

In the game you have to level up and gather a team to fight some enemy in a tower. Whats cool is everytime you play the quest are different, you don't have to play the same quest in the same order, I really like that. The tutorial can be a little missleading, it makes it seem like you just go from map to map without making any decisions, but that's just the game hand walking you through the tutorial. Once you get out the tutorial the ganme opens up and you have to make choices on where you go, and how you apporch your quest. You can collec loot too. It's very simple and basic game system, but thats what I love about it. 

If you're looking for a fun offline solo rpg, with procedurally generated quest, you should buy this soon as you can

They patched it, Successor went from looking kinda bad to one of the better strategy games on quest!

It is a solid title, the strategy is fun and somewhat demanding at times. The story is ok, so far. The graphics can be quite beautiful. Most importantly for a game like this the UI works, presentation is good and the VR mechanics are fairly solid(Grit and Valor could learn from this!

It does still contain a bunch of bugs and graphical glitches and is in need of more improvements. Please keep working on this.

This game suddenly appeared out of nowhere without any marketing, and it would be a shame if it went unnoticed, because there aren't many good VR RPGs. I didn't expect Successor to be so addictive, it absorbed me for hours. I really like the game mechanics, the characters and their skills and the fact that the battles are very tactical and require a lot of planning. Additionally, it is a roguelike and if you lose a battle you have to start the entire campaign over again, which makes you have to play carefully and the gameplay more exciting. It's great that it has a campaign editor, so after completing all six campaigns we can generate our own adventures. Sure, there are a few things that could be improved, there are some bugs, the skill trees are unclear, and the game's graphics have taken a significant downgrade compared to the flat version, but it still brought me many hours of fun. I recommend it to fans of RPGs and miniatures board games :)

An epic strategy game. Simple to understand and fun to play. The XR elements adds to the experience. Don't miss out!


Thanks a lot to the devs for fixing issues and keeping this game updated!

I can recommend this game for its gameplay... It got a lot of content and it's fun. However the graphics are seriously lacking for a quest 3 title. This brand new game looks like it has been  super optimized for quest 2 support. There is no graphics toggle to enhance graphics. There is no PC VR version either with enhanced graphics. The texture quality is very low in many places. Those barrel textures in the war room are the worst I've seen. You can see how the 3d models of objects and characters don't look right because they have been optimized.By the looks of it, the graphic optimization was automated. No fluid motion in bodies of water, No fog,  no shaders, no poison clouds, no auras.. shaders anyone? The graphics of this game are so dull and bland. The devs need to add a graphics option for quest 3 quality and a PC VR version with similar graphics to the stand alone PC version.There is also a bug that causes your 4th party member not to appear at mission start randomly. There is also a bug window that appears when a mission is finished that is about clicking on it with a mouse... Part of the PC port.  Good game, lame VR port...

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and detailed feedback! 
We’ve just released a visual update that significantly improves overall resolution and preserves character detail across distances. We’re continuing to explore specific enhancements and ways to raise visual fidelity without hurting performance.

reviews.review_description:
Negatives:

Optimization needs work: when you zoom out, the game gets blurry instead of sharp.

Mixed Reality bug: there’s an odd line in front of you, and if the board crosses it, it disappears. Pretty frustrating since MR is my go-to mode for this type of game.


Positives:

The combat system is great. Managing multiple characters with unique abilities is fun and keeps the gameplay fresh.

The devs clearly want to push the game further: some interactive elements in the environment add to the immersion (still simple—like weapons or torches—but it’s a good start).

Big plus: it’s fully single-player, which I really enjoy.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review.
Apart from the visual updates we released a while ago, we just released a hotfix that removes the clipping plane, which rendered 3D elements invisible near or behind the player's body. Make sure to update your Successor app to version 1.0.0.261 or above.

reviews.review_description: The game keeps locking up, the lettering is way too small, it is taking forever to get through the levels the voices are too low to hear, I mean, don't the developer ever play their games first? It needs a lot of work. It could be a great game with better graphics and sound.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts. We play Successor daily, but as developers, we sometimes get blind to obvious issues, so feedback is really valuable.

You’re absolutely right about the voice levels; they’re too low at a distance, and we’ll be correcting that. If you have a moment, we’d love to know more about when the game locks up and what you mean by the levels taking forever. This would help us pinpoint any issues. You can reach us directly at support@playwoodgames.com or join the discussion on our Discord: https://www.wartile.com/JoinOurDiscord

reviews.review_description: This game just came out and I can already see the vast potential it has. You can tell it needs a little polish but I already love it. For lovers of Demeo, do not overlook this title. And for the developer, please keep working on and evolving this awesome game. You have a gem on your hands!

This game is immersive in its own way, a rich storyline coupled with surround sound in battle to the tiny detail of weapons falling off dead soldiers, this game has it all. Enjoying the game so far!

As you would see in other reviews, graphics isn’t the best, but as a strategy game fan, graphics come after the gameplay and planning elements. 
Hope the developers will continue to update the game !

I’m really enjoying this.
You start by doing a tutorial, and then onto the story campaign.

You move pieces around a hexagon battlefield, stamina is used to perform special actions for each character.
You find chests on each battlefield… remember to unlock them before finishing the battle! The chests give coins and scrolls, that are used to upgrade your characters skills.

On the campaign, you move around a map, with random encounters which can give you random items, or even give you bad events for your next battles, such as spider attacks… which sometimes you can use to your advantage.

Each battle on the map, you can look at and it tells you the difficulty and what rewards you will get from completing the battle.
These rewards can be equipped by your various characters, to make them stronger, tougher or improve their skills and stats with magical items.

During battles you have to think ahead, using skills from a distance, and higher ground to your advantage… also use obstacles to help defend against the enemy.. or fire from behind.

I am really enjoying this game, and time flew when I was playing it!

It seems a little easy so far though, with battles marked as strong or very strong, I was finding them fairly easy… but maybe later in the game, they become more challenging.

Graphics are good, with the ability to move the battle area around, up and down and zoom right into the action.. or zoom out for a tactical view.

Sounds are good too, so is the medieval style music.

This is a great game for anyone, but aimed more at RTS, or TBS game fans.

I’m very happy to recommend this great game!… well done developers!

The gameplay takes a little bit to get used to, but once that happens, the game is a breeze and a lot of fun to play. I just started so I’ll let you know more as I get along.the graphics are great anyone who says different is just g too demanding the Quest Optimiser app helps

Great game played the first mission so far. Looks good. The game play is good . I love the mixed reality mode. 
Well done 👏 ✔️ 👍

You can pause at any time with B button and issue new commands to each of your units by laser pointing at them then pressing the A button.

Seems to have quite a decent amount of depth, with loot you can fit in 1 of 3 slots for each character you get (armour , weapon and trinket).   There are two sort of map - one of the whole world/realm in hexes and then a separate diorama for each of the encounters that you have in some of those hexes.   Personally I think the text resolution, graphics, music and animation are quite good.

However Im getting a weird bug where I can only play it when facing one direction or a large block of the diorama becomes transparent (apart from a very thin outline).  I can  also see a bright green cube with the word "table" on it in the background which is offputting.   No other reviews mention this so im wondering if its some sort of conflict with some very old setup I remember doing for meta roomscale which added adding furniture (no idea if that still exists).

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the review and kind words! 🙏
We’ve improved the overall resolution in the latest update and removed the green "table" cube. The clipping issue behind the player is still under investigation, and we hope to roll out a fix soon.

reviews.review_description: This game is fantastic. Addictive gameplay, great combat mechanics, and an extensive campaign system. The only downside to this game is the disappearing graphics in mixed reality mode.
If you love Demeo, you will enjoy this!
Why i cant save progress starts all the way from beginning..,why

developer_response.body:
Hi :)
The first tutorial in the Betrayal campaign takes about 10–15 minutes to finish. It has four boards, but it's treated as one big battle. The game doesn't save during battles, and this is not communicated. Sorry about that. However, once you finish the tutorial, the game will automatically save your progress after each battle, every move you make on the region map, and when you quit the game.

reviews.review_description:
Early impressions review

Just got into this today ￼and loving what I’m seeing and playing so far. 
The diorama is just amazing, you can spin it, make it huge as you want and bring it small.
The miniature world is stunning,  from the moment you dive in this game oozes class.
One of the best looking table top games out there.
I’m new to this type of game but so keen to learn everything.
Congratulations to the devs, this is going to be an epic ride.
This game is a true hidden gem and is a masterpiece for its class.I can't say enough about it.It's been a great pleasure, and I highly recommend it.

developer_response.body:
Hi, we are very sorry to hear about this issue. Sadly, Quest 3 has a bug with an invisible plane in MR. Until we have it fixed, there is a workaround described in our latest update (With Ruler Crowns). You can find our Discord here - https://www.wartile.com/JoinOurDiscord. We would love to help if we can. 

Best regards
The Playwood Project Dev Team
